summary: Matched the common fighter CargoFall action by moving ftCo_8009BC58 and the CargoFall Anim/IASA/Phys/Coll callbacks out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into new C/header files. The PR also introduced a CargoLanding file/header for the ftCo_8009BD4C collision landing callback, but kept that callback as inline ASM under MUST_MATCH/WIP guards with NOT_IMPLEMENTED in the non-matching path. Build wiring in obj_files.mk was updated and ftCo_8009BC58's declaration moved from CargoJump to CargoFall. The report showed src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoFall.c at 100% with all listed functions matched.
what_changed: Removed the ftCo_8009BC58, ftCo_CargoFall_Anim, ftCo_CargoFall_IASA, ftCo_CargoFall_Phys, ftCo_CargoFall_Coll, and local ftCo_8009BD4C assembly block from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoFall.c implementing the CargoFall entry/action callbacks in C.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoFall.h with prototypes and address comments for ftCo_8009BC58 and the four CargoFall callbacks.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_CargoLanding.c and ftCo_CargoLanding.h for ftCo_8009BD4C, including constants ftCo_804D8738 = 0 and ftCo_804D873C = 1.
Kept ftCo_8009BD4C as an ASM function when MUST_MATCH is enabled, with a NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub for the non-MUST_MATCH or WIP path.
Updated obj_files.mk to build ftCo_CargoFall.c.o and ftCo_CargoLanding.c.o before the remaining ft_08A4.s object.
Moved the ftCo_8009BC58 declaration out of ftCo_CargoJump.h and included ftCo_CargoFall.h from ftCo_CargoWait.c.
smart_moves: Split CargoFall declarations into their own header instead of leaving ftCo_8009BC58 declared in CargoJump, improving action-file ownership and discoverability.
Used existing fighter action helpers directly: Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_SetAnimRate, ftCommon_8007D5D4, ft_80084DB0, ft_80082C74, and ftCo_8009C5A4.

Preserved the harder collision transition callback ftCo_8009BD4C as inline ASM while still relocating it into a thematically named CargoLanding unit, allowing the CargoFall file itself to reach a full match without forcing all neighboring logic to be decompiled at once.
Captured small float constants formerly in sdata2 as C constants in the new CargoLanding file.
Used a MUST_MATCH-only dummy stack array in ftCo_8009BC58 to satisfy matching constraints for unused stack behavior without polluting normal builds.
decomp_lessons: When an action's main callbacks are matchable but a related callback is not, it is acceptable to peel the matched action into C and carry the remaining callback as guarded inline ASM in a more appropriate source file.
Headers should follow action ownership: ftCo_8009BC58 belongs with CargoFall rather than CargoJump once the code is split out.

Action callback patterns can be very small wrappers: an empty Anim callback, an IASA callback that only RETURN_IFs a helper, a Phys callback that delegates to ft_80084DB0, and a Coll callback that delegates to ft_80082C74 with a landing callback.
For common fighter cargo state transitions, x2CC->x4_motion_state offsets are used to select related motion states; this PR used +6 for CargoFall and the landing ASM used +8.
MUST_MATCH stack padding can be isolated with preprocessor guards and documented as an unused-stack TODO.
naming_conventions: Cargo action files follow ftCo_Cargo<Action>.c and ftCo_Cargo<Action>.h, e.g. ftCo_CargoFall and ftCo_CargoLanding.
Common fighter symbols use the ftCo_ prefix, with named callbacks formatted as ftCo_CargoFall_Anim, ftCo_CargoFall_IASA, ftCo_CargoFall_Phys, and ftCo_CargoFall_Coll.
Unidentified functions keep address-based names such as ftCo_8009BC58 and ftCo_8009BD4C, with address comments in headers.
Header include guards in this slice used GALE01_<address>, e.g. GALE01_09BC58 and GALE01_09BD4C.
Motion state enum naming seen in the implementation includes ftCo_MS_ShoulderedWait.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted the matched CargoFall assembly from ft_08A4.s after adding the C object files to obj_files.mk.
Used `#ifdef MUST_MATCH` with `u8 _[16] = { 0 };` in ftCo_8009BC58 to reproduce unused stack layout.
Used `ASM void ... { nofralloc ... }` plus `#pragma peephole on` for ftCo_8009BD4C, preserving exact assembly while moving it out of the monolithic asm file.
Provided a non-matching fallback body with NOT_IMPLEMENTED for the ASM-only ftCo_8009BD4C path.
Represented former sdata2 zero/one constants as `float const` definitions in C so the relocated inline ASM could reference ftCo_804D8738 and ftCo_804D873C.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully decompile ftCo_8009BD4C or remove its inline ASM from ftCo_CargoLanding.c.
Search for other Cargo action PRs that use x2CC->x4_motion_state offsets for motion state transitions.
Look for additional uses of MUST_MATCH-only unused stack arrays in fighter action matching.
Compare ftCo_CargoJump, ftCo_CargoFall, and ftCo_CargoLanding callback patterns for reusable common cargo-state structure.
